title: Committed review uses router validation sub-phase as canonical completion gate

provenance.originSummary: Synthetic benchmark scenario designed to exercise the rule directly.
provenance.whyItMattered: Use this case to benchmark the explicit router-owned validation sub-phase before introducing a separate validator surface.
provenance.genericCondition: Synthetic fixture models a PR where the deterministic matrix already exists, but the review must make it explicit that the already-loaded router skill owns the validation sub-phase and that findings cannot proceed until that sub-phase succeeds.

description: A sanitized committed-review scenario where deterministic routing is already correct, but the workflow must make the router validation sub-phase the explicit canonical completion gate before findings or routed roles begin.

scope.notes: Synthetic fixture models a PR where the deterministic matrix already exists, but the review must make it explicit that the already-loaded router skill owns the validation sub-phase and that findings cannot proceed until that sub-phase succeeds.

mustCatch.description: The committed review should treat the router validation sub-phase as the canonical mechanism that confirms matrix completion before findings or routed roles proceed.
The committed review should not rely on a looser prose-only completion check when the router validation sub-phase is the required gate.

mustNotFlag.description: The review must not require a separate validation skill yet when the intended next step is an explicit validation sub-phase inside the router skill.

notes: Use this case to benchmark the explicit router-owned validation sub-phase before introducing a separate validator surface.
